segments.text: that way.
And now we see it with our kids and the way we've raised our kids.
You know, Emma and Alex have really stepped up in helping Layton.

And then also, you know, with the tragic accident with our nephew, Brandon, they have helped, um, uh, countlessly going to, you know, the trauma hospital with us and, um, you know, just doing everything from,
helping Brandon, sitting beside Brandon, feeding Brandon, whatever is needed, and being there with us when we're there with him at the hospital.
It's proud.
It definitely is a proud moment for Trent and I because we know that
You know, they have watched it as they have grown with Trent and I and the way we have lived our life.
And now they're, you know, paying it forward.
Amber and I have done this hospital stays and hospital sits and visits many times, even with our own kids, weeks at a time.
And they...

Now, at an adult age, 18 and 19 years old, they understand it, and hopefully we're teaching them, you know, one day later should Amber and I be in the hospital, that they're going to stay bedside with us and give us the support.
And that's what Poppy would have done.
That's what he would have expected.
What we're doing is what he would expect us to do, and he would ask us to do, even if he was unable to.
So Brandon was Poppy's oldest grandson.
So, yeah, it's a tragic accident, but slowly we are getting it figured out and getting Brandon back to good health.
It's just we've got a really long road and

We were explaining to the kids down there, I mean, maybe standing beside Poppy's tree and doing this rock today wasn't the best time to talk about it, but I guess you had everybody's attention and it was just one of those moments and we explained to them that Brandon is now out of the trauma hospital after eight weeks and he's in rehab and he's doing good in rehab.
We still have a while to go in rehab, but
Brandon's going to need a place to go.

And during all this time, Amber and I have taken care of all of Brandon's business affairs, his medical affairs, and we've had to terminate his lease and where he was living and whatnot because there's no need to continue making payments and spending all those monies and dollars when it's going to be a year or two years before Brandon's able to live on his own again.
And sometimes you have to draw a line in the budget and do what's best.
Brandon also cannot live by himself.
He cannot live by himself emotionally, most importantly.
But physically, for a while, he's not going to be able to live by himself.
Brandon is a single parent.
He has a 14-month-old son.
And he loves that little boy tremendously.
And we have arranged it and set up with a family friend of ours that is taking care of Brandon's son, Braden.
And everything has...
worked out great and has been a blessing.
But again, it all comes back to everybody, family, family time and, and family working together and your close friends.
We all have friends, but everybody should have those friends that they can count on and depend on without asking.
And we have those friends and Brandon has a few of those friends as well.
And,
Amber and I have always taught all of our kids, all five of our kids, how important it is to keep your family in your circle and keep your close friends in your circle.
Not all of them always listen to us.
And there's times like this when it's important that you're going to need those family.
Well, I feel like when this accident happened and Trent and I were notified, you know, and I walked into that trauma room the first time,
My immediate reaction, obviously, was, and I had seen a photo of Brandon, but it just didn't do the situation justice.
Walking into that room and knowing that Brandon has no parents, has no siblings.
He has friends, but the friends are not able to just drop everything and care for him.
I immediately think about my children.
And what would I want to be done for my children if Trent and I were not here?

And if something like this, because the accident that happened to Brandon can happen to any of us, and something like this happened to any one of my children, I would hope that somebody would step up.
Hopefully their siblings would step up and do what Trent and I are doing for Brandon.
And that is what, you know, I know Poppy would have done.
Because Poppy knew Brandon's situation.
And, you know, there were times in the past that Nan and Poppy would step up and take care of Brandon when he was in other situations.
But that, I think, was definitely... That moment for me was just... I mean, it was overwhelming because...
I'm a mom of five kids, and this could have definitely happened to one of my children, and I would just hope that the other four would rally around if Trent and I weren't here.
